title: Natural deodorant paste
subtitle: A bicarbonate and arrowroot paste in coconut oil and shea butter
excerpt: A natural deodorant paste made from bicarbonate of soda, arrowroot powder, coconut oil, shea butter, and lavender essential oil. Anhydrous and preservative-free. Applied with a fingertip; the coconut oil melts on contact and the bicarbonate neutralises odour-causing bacteria.
sourceNotes: Bramble Berry deodorant paste formulation notes (brambleberry.com, May 2026). Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014), p. 327, lavender in leave-on underarm products.

content.text: A natural deodorant paste. The active ingredient is bicarbonate of soda, which neutralises the short-chain fatty acids produced by axillary bacteria (the compounds that smell). Arrowroot powder absorbs surface moisture without the drawing effect of clay. Coconut oil and shea butter form the carrier base; the paste is

Bicarbonate of soda at high strength can cause a rash on some skin types. If redness or itching shows after a few days, drop the bicarbonate from 15% to 8% and top up with more arrowroot. The lower-bicarb paste is kinder on sensitive skin but less effective on high-sweat days.

items.name: Coconut oil
items.prepNote: refined coconut oil. 45% of the batch. The melting base; melt point around 24°C, so the paste softens and melts on skin contact.

items.name: Shea butter
items.prepNote: refined shea butter. 20% of the batch. Adds skin feel and raises the melt point slightly.

items.name: Bicarbonate of soda
items.prepNote: fine bicarbonate of soda (baking soda). 15% of the batch. The odour-control active. Reduce to 8 g for a sensitive-skin version.

items.name: Arrowroot powder (cosmetic)
items.prepNote: cosmetic-grade arrowroot starch. 18% of the batch. Absorbs surface moisture without astringency.

items.name: Lavender essential oil
items.prepNote: Lavandula angustifolia lavender essential oil. 2% of the batch. Provides fragrance and has mild antibacterial properties at use concentration.

content.text: Place the coconut oil and shea butter in a heatproof bowl over barely simmering water. Stir until both are fully melted. Remove from the heat and allow to cool to below 45°C before adding the powders.
Add the powders and essential oil

Weigh the bicarbonate and arrowroot together into a small bowl and stir to combine. Sift through a fine sieve into the melted butter base in a steady sprinkle, stirring continuously to prevent lumps. The mixture will thicken as the powders absorb into the fats. When the powders are fully incorporated and the blend is smooth, add the essential oil and stir through.

Pour or spoon the paste into small tins. Leave at room temperature for 30 minutes until set. The paste will solidify to a soft, smooth consistency. If the room is warm above 25°C, place the tins in the fridge for 10-15 minutes to help them set.

Scoop a pea-sized amount onto a clean dry fingertip and rub into each underarm. The coconut oil melts immediately on skin contact and the paste absorbs within 30 seconds. Apply to dry skin only; wet skin dilutes the bicarbonate and reduces effectiveness. Wait 2 minutes before dressing.

Apply a small amount to the inside of the wrist for 24-48 hours before underarm use. Bicarbonate of soda at 15% is alkaline (pH ~9) and can cause a contact rash in some people, especially those who have recently shaved the underarm area. If redness or itching appears after 2-3 days of use, reduce the bicarb to 8% and increase the arrowroot correspondingly.

12 months in a cool, dry place. The paste softens above 25°C because of the coconut oil base, so keep it in a cool cupboard in summer. Keep the tin lid on when not in use to keep it clean. Water-free, so no preservative is needed.
